content:
# Daily Equipment Maintenance Checklist

**Tool ID:** T180-01
**Block:** B180 (Machine Maintenance Log)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Head Barista
**Time Required:** 10–15 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

Your espresso machine is the most expensive piece of equipment in the cafe and the source of most of your revenue. A blocked group head, a failing pump, or a scaled-up boiler does not just break down — it breaks down during the Saturday morning rush when you can least afford it. This daily checklist catches problems early, extends equipment life, and keeps your coffee tasting right.

Ten minutes at opening, five at closing. That is all it takes to prevent a EUR 2,000 repair bill.

**Built for Head Barista working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

A daily equipment maintenance checklist covering all critical cafe machinery — espresso machine, grinders, blender, dishwasher, refrigeration, and ovens. Tracks cleaning tasks, condition observations, and early warning signs of equipment failure.

This tool supports **Machine Maintenance Log** (B180) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Head Barista (opening shift) and closing team member
**Time Required:** 10–15 minutes at opening; 5–10 minutes at closing
**Frequency:** Every day the cafe is open. No exceptions.

**What You'll Need:**
- This checklist (laminated at the bar or printed daily)
- Cleaning supplies: Cafiza/Puly Caff, grinder brush, group head brush, steam wand cloth
- Probe thermometer
- Pen or whiteboard marker

**Steps:**
1. Complete the Opening section during morning prep
2. Complete the Closing section at end of service
3. Note any issues in the Fault/Observation column
4. Escalate any RED items to the manager immediately
5. File completed checklists weekly for maintenance records

---

## Checklist

### Date: ________ | Opening: ________ | Closing: ________

### OPENING — Espresso Machine

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| Machine powered on, boiler pressure stable (check gauge: 1.0–1.2 bar) | [ ] | |
| Flush each group head for 10 seconds | [ ] | |
| Check group head gaskets — water flowing evenly, no spraying | [ ] | |
| Shower screens clean — no old coffee residue visible | [ ] | |
| Portafilter baskets clean — holes clear, no blockage | [ ] | |
| Steam wand: purge, check steam pressure, wipe clean | [ ] | |
| Drip tray emptied and cleaned | [ ] | |
| Water softener / filtration: check indicator light or salt level | [ ] | |
| Pull 2–3 test shots — flow rate, colour, and crema normal? | [ ] | |

### OPENING — Grinders

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| House grinder: hopper clean, beans loaded, dial-in complete | [ ] | |
| Decaf grinder: hopper clean, beans loaded, dial-in complete | [ ] | |
| Brush out retained grounds from each grinder chute | [ ] | |
| Check burr sharpness — any channelling in shots, uneven grind? | [ ] | |
| Guest/SO grinder: loaded and dialled in (if applicable) | [ ] | |

### OPENING — Other Equipment

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| Blender: blades clean, jug seated correctly, test run | [ ] | |
| Dishwasher: turned on, water temperature reaching 60C+ wash / 82C+ rinse | [ ] | |
| Fridge: temperature reading _____ C (must be 1–5 C) | [ ] | |
| Freezer: temperature reading _____ C (must be -18 C or below) | [ ] | |
| Oven / toaster: pre-heated to correct temperature, clean interior | [ ] | |
| POS system: powered on, receipt paper loaded, delivery tablet charged | [ ] | |

### CLOSING — Espresso Machine

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| Backflush each group head with Cafiza/Puly Caff (5 cycles per group) | [ ] | |
| Remove and soak shower screens in cleaning solution (15 min) | [ ] | |
| Remove portafilter baskets and soak in cleaning solution | [ ] | |
| Clean portafilter handles — remove coffee oils | [ ] | |
| Steam wand: soak tip in hot water, wipe, purge | [ ] | |
| Drip tray removed, cleaned, replaced | [ ] | |
| Machine exterior wiped down (no cleaning product on group heads) | [ ] | |
| Reassemble shower screens and baskets after soaking | [ ] | |

### CLOSING — Grinders

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| Empty remaining beans from hoppers into sealed containers (label with date) | [ ] | |
| Brush out all retained grounds | [ ] | |
| Wipe exterior and dosing area | [ ] | |
| Check: any unusual noise during today's service? | [ ] | |

### CLOSING — Other Equipment

| Task | Done | Fault / Observation |
|------|------|-------------------|
| Blender: disassemble, wash all parts, air dry | [ ] | |
| Dishwasher: run empty final cycle, clean filter trap, wipe door seals | [ ] | |
| Fridge: check temperature reading _____ C, wipe door handles | [ ] | |
| Oven: turned off, interior wiped when cool | [ ] | |
| Ice machine: check ice level, wipe exterior (if applicable) | [ ] | |

### Weekly Deep-Clean Tasks (mark day completed)

| Task | Mon | Tue | Wed | Thu | Fri | Sat | Sun |
|------|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
| Grinder burr cleaning (remove and brush) | | | [ ] | | | | |
| Espresso machine descale check | | | | | [ ] | | |
| Fridge deep clean (shelves, drawers) | [ ] | | | | | | |
| Dishwasher deep clean (filter, spray arms) | | | | [ ] | | | |
| Blender gasket inspection | | [ ] | | | | | |

### Fault Escalation

**Escalate IMMEDIATELY if any of these are observed:**
- Espresso machine boiler pressure outside 0.8–1.5 bar range
- Water leaking from any machine component
- Grinder making unusual grinding or clicking noise
- Fridge temperature above 8 C (food safety risk)
- Dishwasher not reaching 82 C rinse temperature
- Any electrical sparking, burning smell, or tripped circuit breaker

| Fault | Reported To | Date/Time | Resolution |
|-------|------------|-----------|------------|
| | | | |
| | | | |

### Sign-Off

| Shift | Name | Time | Signature |
|-------|------|------|-----------|
| Opening | | | |
| Closing | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Skipping the Backflush at Closing

**Why it happens:** End of a long shift, everyone wants to go home, "we'll do it tomorrow."
**How to prevent:** The backflush takes 3 minutes per group head. Make it the very first closing task, not the last. No exceptions. Coffee oils left overnight become rancid and taint every drink the next morning.
**If it's already happening:** Taste the first shot tomorrow morning. If it tastes bitter or ashy, rancid oils are the cause. Start backflushing tonight.

### Risk 2: Not Recording Temperature Readings

**Why it happens:** Staff glance at the fridge, say "feels cold," and tick the box. No actual reading taken.
**How to prevent:** Require the actual number written on the checklist. "Tick" without a number is not a valid check. Use a digital thermometer with an external display so the reading is instant.
**If it's already happening:** Buy a fridge thermometer today. VMVT inspectors will ask for temperature records — you need actual numbers.

### Risk 3: Ignoring Early Warning Signs

**Why it happens:** A slight leak, a small noise, a minor pressure fluctuation — "it's probably fine." It is not fine. It is a breakdown waiting for the worst possible moment.
**How to prevent:** Any observation in the "Fault" column must be escalated the same day. No "we'll keep an eye on it." Small problems become expensive emergencies.
**If it's already happening:** Review the last 2 weeks of checklists. Any recurring observations? Act on them today.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add tasks for filter brew equipment (Kalita, V60, batch brewer), cold brew systems, and any specialty equipment unique to your setup.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align with brand-mandated maintenance schedules. Use this checklist to cover tasks the brand schedule misses.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Reduce to espresso machine, grinder, and fridge only. Add generator/power system checks if mobile.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add oven calibration check, proofer temperature, and dough mixer maintenance tasks.

**Making it yours:**
- Add your specific equipment make/model names for clarity
- Attach manufacturer maintenance guides to this checklist
- Include your equipment service company's phone number for emergencies
- Adjust weekly deep-clean days to match your quietest trading days

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T180-02 — Equipment Register (L2, tracker)
- T180-03 — Scheduled Maintenance Calendar (L2, template)
- T180-04 — Equipment Fault Log (L2, tracker)
- T180-05 — Capital Equipment Replacement Planner (L3, template)

**Related blocks:**

**Parent block:** B180 — Machine Maintenance Log
**Module:** M14: Operational Rhythm & Daily Discipline
---

*Tool Version 1.0 — OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
